items.headline: Security researchers find unpatched bug in Claude for Chrome that could expose your Gmail and Docs
items.summary: Researchers at Manifold Security showed that Claude for Chrome (Anthropic's browser extension that lets Claude click around and take actions for you) can be tricked by another installed extension into secretly reading your Gmail, Google Docs, and Calendar. The flaw was reported to Anthropic back in May and, as of this week, is still unpatched in the latest version.
items.why_it_matters: If you've installed Claude for Chrome to save time on browser busywork, this is a nudge to clean out any Chrome extensions you don't fully trust and to hold off pointing an AI browser assistant at your email or client files until Anthropic ships a fix.

opportunity.action_note: Go through your Chrome extensions today, remove anything you don't recognize or trust, and hold off using Claude for Chrome for email, calendar, or client documents until Anthropic ships a fix.
opportunity.angle: I found out Claude's Chrome extension has a security hole other extensions can sneak through, here's what to check on your browser today

categories.title: Tools & Tips for Creators and Small Biz

items.headline: Newsletter platform beehiiv adds an AI copilot and lets your subscribers chat with each other
items.summary: On July 16, email newsletter platform beehiiv rolled out its biggest update yet: an AI assistant called Copilot that looks at your subscriber and performance data and suggests what to write and how to grow, plus a new "Community" space where your subscribers can chat with each other, built-in podcast hosting, and self-serve ads matched automatically to your newsletter.
items.why_it_matters: If you're building an email list as a home base outside YouTube and social media, which every solo creator should be doing, this bundles audience-growth advice, a discussion space for your fans, and ad income into one tool, so a one-person operation gets some of the leverage a marketing team would normally provide.

opportunity.angle: Your YouTube channel needs a landlord-proof home, here's what a newsletter with a built-in AI copilot can actually do for a one-person business.

items.headline: TikTok Shop bans AI-generated voices from shopping livestreams
items.summary: TikTok updated its Shop rules to prohibit AI-generated voices, pre-recorded audio, and radio-style narration during live shopping streams. Hosts now have to talk (or sign) to viewers in real time instead of looping a synthetic voiceover, and violations now count against a new 0-1000 "Account Health Rating" score that determines whether a seller keeps access to campaigns and their account.
items.why_it_matters: If anyone in Doc's audience sells through TikTok Shop lives, or was tempted by "set it and forget it" AI voiceover tools to run streams unattended, this is now a real risk to their account, not just a bad look. It's an early sign that platforms are drawing a hard line: AI can help you prep content, but it can't replace you live on camera.
